editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
P41/26/1 Dear Bill, I was very pleased to get your
letter a few days ago. Really I am ashamed
for having neglected writing for such a long time
but “ when I am bad I am very bad “ you know.
All here are quite well. I suppose you have
heard that Kathleen did not come back to Kerry
after the summer holidays , so now I have to
keep a maid. She is from Mollie's place
and on the whole she is quite all right. I was
at Cferg on Sun. last. They are all first class
there. We met the whole family except the
agricultural Dick. He was on holidays somwhere
as usual. He is as big an idler as ever. Mick got married on Tuesday last. He had
a big turn out - something like fourteen motors
? ? got ?
the wedding I _______ the ___
day, but I didn't see him. I'm sure he is as
fat as ever. Ciss went off on Wed. last, but I haven't heard
a word since. In fact I haven't seen her for
nearly three months. Oh by the way, there
was a great round up here to day don't
get excited - it was only the dogs this time.
The Civic Guards made a swoop on B . . .
& found umpteen dogs without a licence - three or
four at Griffins, & horses, two at Denbys, one at
Julie Egan's & one at ? Sheehy's. I don't know
how Mrs Sullivan fared. They have eleven in all
& I'm sure they paid only for four. Mrs Fluffy
has lost an eye. You should see her now. She
is uglier than ever. Libby is in greta form. Its
well that she needn't go on the run. She is the
nicest little dog of the whole bunch. Your mother hasn't yet got that . . . bill
I'd love to get one & I shall send you the thread
3 the first chance I get. I heard that you have
a picture frame for me. I hope you don't let
it go astray. What skilled workers you'll be
when you come home. Susie hasn't yet got a permanent place
but I'm sure one will turn up soon. Nellie &
. . . have grown very tall & Nora is a
real stack. She was very pleased with your
little medal. I gave her a cuffing ?
for not writing to you. Mrs Maurice went to
Dublin this morning. She took Mickie to a doctor
again. The poor kid wasn't too l well lately. There is nothing strange here. The
folks around often enquire for you & wonder
when you'll return. With all best wishes from
your fond cousin Kit ps
I shall send on a cake to morrow or
after. I had no letter from Jim.
K
